evidence-binding: n1-version-key-isstepdone 89f368f823df

## RED â€” test failure before fix

Test T11 was added to `scripts/test-release.js`. Running against the unfixed `isStepDone` (which only matched on `step` and `status`, not `version`) produced:

```
FAIL: T11: result:ok claimed but 5.2.0 tag is absent â€” fabricated-pass detected; tags=""
FAIL: T11: result:ok claimed but package.json still at 5.1.0 â€” fabricated-pass detected
FAIL: T11: result:ok claimed but CHANGELOG does not contain [5.2.0] â€” fabricated-pass detected

test-release: 3 test(s) FAILED, 49 passed
```

RED: T11 â€” AssertionError (3 failing assertions): --cut 5.2.0 emitted result:ok but 5.2.0 tag absent, package.json at 5.1.0, CHANGELOG missing [5.2.0] (fabricated-pass confirmed pre-fix)

## GREEN â€” test passing after fix

After adding the `version` parameter to `isStepDone` and stamping `version` on all receipt rows:

```
test-release: all 52 assertions passed
```

GREEN: T11 passes; 52/52 assertions green (7 new T11 assertions + 45 pre-existing all green)

## Summary of changes

### scripts/kaola-workflow-release.js (canonical)

- `isStepDone(receipt, step)` â†’ `isStepDone(receipt, step, version)`: added `&& r.version === version` to the matcher so stale receipt rows from a different version are ignored.
- All 5 call sites in `runCut` updated to pass `version` as the third argument: `changelog`, `package_json`, `codex_manifest_N` (Ă—3), `readme`, `git_tag`.
- `git_tag` receipt row now also stamps `version` (in addition to `tag`) so `isStepDone(receipt, 'git_tag', version)` can find it for crash-resume of a partially-completed run.

### plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-release.js (codex plugin â€” byte-identical)

Propagated by `npm run sync:editions` (edition-sync.js --write, COMMON_SCRIPTS copy). Now byte-identical to canonical.

### plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-release.js (gitlab â€” rename-normalized)

Regenerated by applying `renameNormalize(canonical, 'gitlab')` â€” replaces `kaola-workflow-<NAME>` with `kaola-gitlab-workflow-<NAME>` in header comment and usage string. All `isStepDone` fix changes propagated.

### plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-release.js (gitea â€” rename-normalized)

Regenerated by applying `renameNormalize(canonical, 'gitea')`. Same propagation as gitlab edition.

### scripts/test-release.js (test suite)

Added T11 regression test that:
1. Runs `--cut 5.1.0` to completion in a fresh temp git repo.
2. Prepends a new `[Unreleased]` section to CHANGELOG and adds a commit (without clearing the receipt).
3. Runs `--cut 5.2.0` against the stale receipt.
4. Asserts that if `result:ok` is returned, the 5.2.0 tag actually exists, `package.json` says `5.2.0`, and CHANGELOG contains `[5.2.0]`. Fabricated-pass is explicitly detected and fails.

## Sync verification output

```
$ node scripts/edition-sync.js --check

$ node scripts/validate-script-sync.js
OK: 21 common scripts, 30 byte-identical groups, 4 rename-normalized families, and 1 config/hooks.json family in sync.

$ node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js
Workflow walkthrough simulation passed

$ node scripts/test-release.js
test-release: all 52 assertions passed
```
